title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Great Barrier Reef is the world’s largest coral reef system, (0) so large that it can be seen from outer space. (9) Although it is a vibrant ecosystem home to thousands of species, it is currently facing unprecedented challenges due to climate change. (10) Such is the sensitivity of coral to temperature that even a slight increase in ocean warmth can trigger widespread bleaching. (11) Provided that global carbon emissions are reduced, there is still hope for the reef to recover over time. (12) Not only do these corals provide a habitat for marine life, but they also protect coastlines from erosion. (13) Regardless of its immense size, the reef is a fragile structure that requires careful management and international protection. (14) Not until recently did scientists fully understand the complex symbiotic relationship between the coral and the algae living within them. (15) In spite of the damage already done, restoration projects are working to cultivate heat-resistant coral strains. (16) Whether these efforts will be enough to save this natural masterpiece remains a major concern for environmentalists. This unique biological treasure serves as a stark reminder of the delicate balance that exists within our planet's oceans.

example.text: The Great Barrier Reef is the world’s largest coral reef system, (0) so large that it can be seen from outer space.
example.explanation: Adverb used for degree before an adjective followed by 'that'.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did scientists... understand').
Part of the prepositional linker 'In spite of'.
Conjunction introducing a doubt or two possibilities.
